

# Platform and App Shell

## E.P.1: macOS Target
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The application must target macOS (OS X) as the primary supported platform.

## E.P.2: Electron App Initialization
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->

The application must have a functioning main process and renderer.

## E.P.3: Default Window
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->

On launch, the main application window must open maximized, but not in fullscreen mode.

## E.P.4: Local Dev Scripts
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


Provide npm scripts to run the app in development mode on macOS.

## E.P.6: App Name
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The application name must be "Clerkly".

# Tooling and Language

## E.T.1: Toolchain Versions
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->

Use Electron v40.0.0, Node.js v25.5.0, and npm v11.8.0 as the required toolchain versions for this project.

## E.T.4: TypeScript Codebase
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


All application code must be written in TypeScript.

## E.T.5: ESLint for TypeScript and React
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The project must use ESLint to lint all TypeScript and TSX sources in main, preload, renderer, and tests.

## E.T.6: Prettier Formatting
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The project must use Prettier as the code formatter and keep formatting rules consistent with ESLint.

## E.T.7: Lint and Format Scripts
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The package scripts must include lint, lint:fix, format, and format:check commands for ESLint and Prettier.

## E.T.8: Linting Scope
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


Linting and formatting must run on source, configuration, and test files under version control.

# UI and Design Reference

## E.U.1: UI Reference Source
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


A Figma-generated UI reference is stored in docs/development/ui_reference and must be used as the visual reference for the app interface.


## E.U.2: Auth Gate Logo Usage
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The authorization screen must render the Logo component with size="md" and showText={true}, matching the UI reference.

## E.U.3: Sidebar Logo Usage
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->

When the sidebar is expanded, it must render the Logo component with size="md" and showText={true}. When collapsed, it must render the Logo component with size="md" and showText={false}.

## E.U.4: Sidebar Tagline Text
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->

The left navigation panel tagline must read "Stay on track" and must be hidden when the sidebar is collapsed.

## E.U.5: Sidebar Settings Placement
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The left navigation panel must place the Settings item in the bottom section of the sidebar.

## E.U.6: Sidebar Collapse Control
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The left navigation panel must provide a user control to collapse and expand the sidebar.

## E.U.7: Sidebar Collapsed Layout
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


When collapsed, the sidebar must reduce its width and show only icons for navigation items.

## E.U.8: Sidebar Toggle Placement
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The sidebar collapse/expand control must not overlap the sidebar logo and must remain fully within the sidebar bounds in both collapsed and expanded states.

## E.U.9: Sidebar Icon Size Consistency
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The sidebar navigation item icons must remain the same size when the sidebar is collapsed.

## E.U.10: Collapsed Sidebar Icon Centering
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


When the sidebar is collapsed, navigation item icons must be centered within their button containers.

# Storage and Migrations

## E.S.1: Local SQLite Storage
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->

Application data must be stored in a local SQLite database.

## E.S.2: Database Location
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The SQLite database must be stored under the app’s userData directory.

## E.S.3: Backup Policy
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->

Backups must be created only before migrations; no periodic backups are required.

## E.S.4: Migration Failure Behavior
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->

If a migration fails, app startup must stop and display an error.

## E.S.5: Database Creation
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


If the database file is missing, the application must create it.

## E.S.6: Schema Migrations
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


If a database exists but its schema is not current, the application must run migrations to bring it to the latest schema.

## E.S.7: Sidebar State Persistence
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The sidebar collapsed/expanded state must be stored in SQLite and restored on startup.

# Auth and OAuth Flow

## E.A.1: Google Auth Gate Screen
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


If the application is not authorized with the user’s Google account, the app must show a single authorization screen and must not show the sidebar menu.

## E.A.2: Google Sign-In Button
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The authorization screen must include a “Sign in with Google” button.

## E.A.3: System Browser Login
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


Clicking the “Sign in with Google” button must open the system browser for Google login.

## E.A.4: Post-Auth Success UI
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->

After successful authorization, the app must return to the application and display the full interface. The authorization screen must remain visible until the OAuth callback indicates success.

## E.A.5: Auth Error Handling
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


If authorization fails, the app must show an error message and keep the Google sign-in button available.

## E.A.6: OAuth Redirect Mechanism
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->

Google OAuth must use a loopback redirect URI in the form "http://127.0.0.1:<port>" or "http://[::1]:<port>" for desktop authorization.

## E.A.7: OAuth Client ID Source
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->

Auth configuration must be defined in src/auth/auth_google.ts as the primary source of truth.

## E.A.8: Authorization Success Criteria
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


Authorization is considered successful only when the OAuth callback includes a "code" parameter.

## E.A.11: Auth Persistence
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The application must persist the authorization state between launches and restore it on startup.

## E.A.12: Encrypted Token Storage
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


OAuth tokens must be stored in SQLite in encrypted form; plaintext token storage is prohibited.

## E.A.13: Silent Token Refresh
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


OAuth access tokens must be refreshed silently in the background when possible.

## E.A.14: Sign-Out
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The UI must provide an explicit sign-out action that clears the stored session and tokens.

## E.A.15: Auth Config Source
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->

Runtime authorization data must be stored in the SQLite database; configuration defaults may be sourced from src/auth/auth_google.ts.

## E.A.16: OAuth PKCE Required
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


Google OAuth must use PKCE (code_verifier/code_challenge with S256) for desktop authorization.

## E.A.18: PKCE Verifier Lifetime
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The PKCE code_verifier must be kept in memory only for the active authorization attempt and cleared after token exchange or failure.

## E.A.19: OAuth State Validation
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


OAuth authorization requests must include a random state value and the callback must validate that the state matches the pending authorization attempt before exchanging the code.

## E.A.20: OAuth Client Secret Usage
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The application must include the OAuth client secret when exchanging authorization codes and refreshing tokens with Google.

## E.A.21: OAuth Client Secret Handling
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->

The OAuth client secret must be defined in src/auth/auth_google.ts and used for token exchange and refresh.

## E.A.22: Auth Gate Retry After Browser Close
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


If the user closes the browser tab during Google authorization, the application must remain in the unauthorized state, keep the “Sign in with Google” button available, and allow starting the authorization flow again.

## E.A.23: Authorization Complete Auto-Close
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The authorization completion page must attempt to close itself with setTimeout(() => window.close(), 300).

## E.A.24: Authorization Completion Branding
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The authorization completion page must render the Clerkly logo and a styled success message while keeping the auto-close behavior.

## E.A.25: Authorization Completion Return Message
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The authorization completion page must mention that the user can return to the Clerkly app.

# IPC and Architecture

## E.I.1: Module Boundary Separation
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The main process, preload script, and renderer must be separated into distinct modules. Cross-layer imports are prohibited; communication between layers must occur only via IPC.

## E.I.2: Auth IPC Contract
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The IPC channels "auth:open-google" and "auth:result" are part of the stable auth contract and must not be changed without updating requirements.

## E.I.3: Sidebar State IPC
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The renderer must read and update sidebar collapsed state via IPC handlers; direct database access from the renderer is prohibited.

# Quality and Logging

## E.Q.1: Error Logging
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


Auth and database errors must be logged to a file under userData, with log rotation by size.

## E.Q.2: Auth/DB Test Coverage
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->

Tests are required; see Testing requirements.

# Testing

## E.TE.1: Unit Coverage for Requirements
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


Each requirement must be covered by unit tests.

## E.TE.2: Test Documentation
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


Each test must include comments specifying: preconditions, action, assertions, and the requirement indices it covers.

## E.TE.3: No Network in Unit Tests
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


Unit tests must not perform network requests.
